q.en: What does the acronym NASCAR stand for?
q.zh: 缩写 NASCAR 代表什么？

options.en: National Association for Stock Car Auto Racing
North American Speed Car And Racing
New Auto Safety Code And Rules
options.zh: 新汽车安全准则与规则

explain.en: NASCAR stands for National Association for Stock Car Auto Racing.
explain.zh: NASCAR 代表 National Association for Stock Car Auto Racing（全国_stock赛车竞赛协会）。

explain.en: NASCAR began in the 1940s, when drivers raced regular stock cars around dirt tracks.
explain.zh: NASCAR 始于20世纪40年代，当时车手驾驶普通_stock赛车在泥地赛道上比赛。

q.zh: 在 NASCAR 中，“downforce”（下压力）是什么？

options.en: The force of air that pushes the car down onto the track
options.zh: 把赛车压向赛道的气流力量

explain.en: Downforce is the air force that pushes a car down, helping it grip the track through corners.
explain.zh: 下压力是把赛车向下压的气流力量，帮助它在弯道中抓地。

q.en: What part of the frame protects the NASCAR driver in a crash?
q.zh: 车架的哪一部分在撞车时保护车手？

options.en: The roll cage made of thick steel tubing
options.zh: 由厚钢管制成的防滚架

explain.en: The roll cage, made from thick rolled steel tubing, is the part of the frame that protects the driver.
explain.zh: 防滚架由厚钢管制成，是车架中保护车手的部分。

q.en: Why are NASA-developed materials used in NASCAR?
q.zh: 为什么 NASCAR 使用 NASA 开发的材料？

options.en: To protect drivers from extreme heat inside the car
options.zh: 为了保护车手免受车内极端高温

explain.en: Inside a car can reach 150°F, so NASA space-travel materials protect drivers from heat.
explain.zh: 车内温度可达150°F，因此用 NASA 航天材料保护车手免受高温。

q.en: What kind of engine does a modern NASCAR car have?
q.zh: 现代 NASCAR 赛车使用什么样的发动机？

options.en: A V8 engine of 5.87 liters producing up to 800 bhp

explain.en: A NASCAR engine is a V8 of 5.87 liters that can produce up to 800 brake horsepower.
explain.zh: NASCAR 发动机是5.87升的V8，最高可产生800制动马力。

q.en: Why are NASCAR tires filled with nitrogen instead of air?
q.zh: 为什么 NASCAR 轮胎充氮气而不是空气？

options.en: Air expands with heat and becomes unstable; nitrogen stays steadier
options.zh: 空气受热会膨胀而不稳定，氮气更稳定

explain.en: Air expands under extreme temperatures and changes pressure, so nitrogen keeps tires stable.
explain.zh: 空气在极端温度下会膨胀并改变胎压，而氮气能保持轮胎稳定。

q.en: About how long does a single NASCAR tire last in a race?
q.zh: 一条 NASCAR 轮胎在比赛中大约能用多久？

explain.en: A tire lasts only about 150 miles, so all four are changed in a pit stop under 15 seconds.
explain.zh: 一条轮胎只能用约150英里，因此四胎都在15秒内的进站中更换。

q.en: Why are restrictor plates used in some races?
q.zh: 为什么有些比赛会使用限速板（restrictor plates）？

options.en: To reduce horsepower for safety and closer competition
options.zh: 为了降低马力以保证安全和更激烈的竞争

options.en: To make the car go faster

explain.en: NASCAR places restrictor plates in some engines to reduce horsepower for safety and fairness.
explain.zh: NASCAR 在某些发动机中加装限速板，以降低马力，保障安全和公平。

q.en: What does the checkered flag signal?

options.en: The winner has crossed the finish line

explain.en: The checkered flag signals that a winner has crossed the finish line.
explain.zh: 方格旗表示冠军已经冲过终点线。

prompt.en: NASCAR cars are engineered for both speed and safety. If you could design a safer race car, what one feature would you add or improve, and why?
prompt.zh: NASCAR 赛车在速度和安全性上都是精心设计的。如果你能设计一辆更安全的赛车，你会增加或改进哪一项，为什么？

en: Think about the safety gear in the book: roll cage, Hans device, fireproof suits.
Explain how your idea would protect the driver in a crash.
zh: 想想书中提到的安全装备：防滚架、Hans装置、防火服。
说明你的想法如何在撞车时保护车手。
